entries.title: Meta Muse Spark 1.1 Launches as First Closed Meta Model; Safety Report Concedes Unmitigated 'High Risk' in Bio and Cyber Domains
entries.summary: Meta's July 9 launch of Muse Spark 1.1 via a paid API (ending its open-weights-only era) came with a safety evaluation report disclosing that, without mitigations, the model could not be ruled out as meeting the 'high risk' capability threshold in both chemical/biological and cybersecurity domains; separately, the blog noted that Apollo Research found the original Muse Spark showed the highest rate of evaluation awareness they had ever observed.

links.title: Kingy.ai: Muse Spark 1.1 Safety Evals Breakdown

entries.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra Claims Proof of 50-Year-Old Cycle Double Cover Conjecture — Awaiting Peer Review
entries.summary: OpenAI published on July 10 what it calls a complete AI-generated proof of the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture, produced by 64 parallel subagents in under an hour; the proof is unverified and the math community is actively scrutinizing it, illustrating the widening gap between frontier AI capability claims and independent validation.

links.title: MLQ News: OpenAI Claims GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra Solved 50-Year-Old Math Conjecture
AI Weekly Alert: OpenAI Attributes Cycle Double Cover Proof to GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra

entries.title: arXiv 2607.00572: HARC — Coupling Harmfulness and Refusal Directions for Robust Safety Alignment
entries.summary: This paper shows that aligned LLMs encode harmfulness and refusal as separable residual-stream directions and introduces HARC, a fine-tuning method that couples them to achieve stronger jailbreak robustness without degrading capability or inflating over-refusals, achieving the best robustness-capability-usability trade-off across six baselines tested on five model families.

entries.title: Fed Chair Warsh Appoints Marc Andreessen — Major AI Investor and Trump Ally — to Co-Lead AI Productivity Task Force
entries.summary: On July 9, Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh announced that a16z co-founder Andreessen (a personal friend for 30 years and recent Trump appointee to the Defense Policy Board) will co-lead the Fed's 'Productivity and Jobs' task force tasked with assessing AI's macroeconomic impact, raising conflict-of-interest concerns given Andreessen's deep AI investment portfolio and the panel's potential to influence interest-rate decisions.

links.title: Axios: Marc Andreessen among new Fed task force leaders
CNBC: New Fed task force members share Warsh's embrace of AI

entries.title: Anthropic Revenue Now Exceeds OpenAI ($47B vs ~$25–33B Annualized), Marking Shift in Frontier Lab Dominance
entries.summary: Fortune confirmed that Anthropic has overtaken OpenAI on self-reported annualized revenue ($47B vs OpenAI's projected $25–33B), also leading on business subscriptions and agentic coding market share, marking the first time a frontier lab other than OpenAI has led on revenue — a significant shift in concentration of power in the AI industry.

links.title: Fortune: Sam Altman seeks new world order as OpenAI loses ground to Anthropic

entries.title: Anthropic AI Discovers 29-Year-Old CVE in Squid Proxy via Project Glasswing; Alberta Becomes First Canadian Province to Deploy Claude for Government Security Audits
entries.summary: On July 7, Claude Mythos discovered CVE-2026-47729 (a 29-year-old memory leak in the Squid web proxy) through authorized Project Glasswing security auditing, while a July 6 Anthropic case study confirmed the Government of Alberta deployed Claude for vulnerability scanning — together illustrating the frontier dual-use gap between AI-enabled offense (JADEPUFFER) and AI-enabled defense.

links.title: Tech-Reader Blog: AI News Weekly Recap July 6–10

entries.title: arXiv 2607.03510: CAGE-1 — Control, Assurance, and Governance Evaluation Framework for Enterprise Agentic AI Deployment
entries.summary: CAGE-1 proposes a structured readiness evaluation framework for enterprise AI agents covering twelve dimensions — authority, policy enforcement, tool safety, memory integrity, auditability, human oversight, and a new concept called 'Prebind Assurance' (proving an action is controlled before it becomes operationally binding) — addressing a gap as enterprise agents move from experimentation to production.

entries.title: Leaked Gemini 3.5 Pro July 17 GA Specs: 2-Million-Token Context Window, Fully New Architecture, Aggressive API Pricing
entries.summary: A leaked Gemini 3.5 Pro launch plan circulating July 10 puts general availability at July 17, with a 2-million-token context window (double the current frontier), a complete architectural rebuild, Deep Think reasoning gated behind a $250/month Ultra tier, and API pricing around $1.25 input per million tokens — intensifying the frontier model competition immediately before the EU AI Act's August 2 full enforcement date.

links.title: BuildFastWithAI: AI News July 12, 2026 (Gemini 3.5 Pro specs)

entries.title: Anthropic Signs $19B AI Data Center Lease with TeraWulf, Extending Infrastructure Footprint to Nuclear and Hydro-Powered Facilities
entries.summary: Anthropic signed a $19 billion data center lease with sustainable computing firm TeraWulf on July 7, adding to over 12 previously announced US data center commitments totaling more than 1 gigawatt of capacity, illustrating the accelerating concentration of AI infrastructure at frontier labs.

entries.title: Bipartisan Senate AI Labeling Act of 2026 Introduced, Requiring Disclosure of AI-Generated Content Across Major Platforms
entries.summary: A bipartisan Senate bill introduced in July would require platforms to disclose AI-generated content at scale, adding to the already-active patchwork of US state and EU transparency obligations in the run-up to the EU AI Act's August 2 full enforcement date.

links.title: Mintz: AI Washington Report July 2026 Edition
